title: Feeding the Pigeons
summary: A boy and his grandpa sit on a Paris park bench and share old bread with the pigeons.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: Grandpa, can I have some bread for the birds?

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Of course, Theo. Here is a small bag. It is old bread from this morning.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: Why old bread? Is fresh bread bad for them?

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Fresh bread is too soft. Old bread is better for the birds.

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Throw small pieces. Not big ones. Their beaks are small.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: Now there are ten! Where are they all coming from?

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: From the church roof. They see the bread and they fly down.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: That one has only one foot!

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Yes, his name is Capitaine. He comes to this bench every Sunday.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: Capitaine the pigeon? You know his name?

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: I see him every week. He always sits near me.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: Look, he is eating from my hand!

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Stay very still. Do not move your fingers.

body.speaker: Theo
body.text: His feathers are grey and a little purple. He is beautiful.

body.speaker: Grandpa
body.text: Yes. Pigeons are friends of cities. Many people forget that.

annotations.exampleEn: Pigeons live in many big cities.
We feed the ducks with old bread.
Cut the apple into small pieces.
Birds use their beaks to eat.
The birds sit on the church roof.
The birds fly down to the ground.
The little bird has only one foot.

annotations.meaningVi: tên riêng (kiểu Pháp, nghĩa là 'thuyền trưởng/đại uý')

annotations.exampleEn: Stay very still and the bird will come.
Her cushion is full of soft feathers.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives, again
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu mệnh lệnh là cách hướng dẫn thẳng thắn nhưng vẫn lịch sự nếu nói với người thân: 'Throw small pieces.' / 'Stay very still.' / 'Do not move your fingers.' Câu phủ định bắt đầu bằng 'Do not'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Imperatives give direct but friendly instructions: 'Throw small pieces.' / 'Stay very still.' / 'Do not move your fingers.' Negative imperatives start with 'Do not'.

grammarNotes.title: Present continuous for things happening now
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi miêu tả việc đang diễn ra trước mắt, ta dùng hiện tại tiếp diễn 'is/are + V-ing': 'He is eating from my hand!' / 'Where are they all coming from?' Đây là điểm ngữ pháp quan trọng ở A1, dùng để báo việc đang xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present continuous 'is/are + V-ing' for actions happening right now: 'He is eating from my hand!' / 'Where are they all coming from?' This A1 structure reports what is happening at the moment.

translationVi: Theo: Ông ơi, cháu xin chút bánh mì cho chim được không ạ? Ông: Được chứ, Theo. Đây là một túi nhỏ. Bánh mì cũ từ sáng nay đó. Theo: Sao lại là bánh cũ ạ? Bánh mì mới có hại cho chim sao? Ông: Bánh mới mềm quá. Bánh cũ tốt hơn cho chim. Theo: Nhìn kìa! Có ba con bồ câu rồi! Ông: Cháu ném từng miếng nhỏ thôi nhé, đừng miếng to. Mỏ chúng nhỏ lắm. Theo: Giờ có tận mười con! Chúng từ đâu ra thế ạ? Ông: Từ mái nhà thờ. Chúng thấy bánh mì là bay xuống. Theo: Con kia có một chân thôi kìa! Ông: Đúng rồi, nó tên là Capitaine. Chủ nhật nào nó cũng đến cái ghế này. Theo: Bồ câu Capitaine á? Ông biết tên nó luôn ạ? Ông: Ông gặp nó mỗi tuần. Nó luôn đậu gần ông. Theo: Nhìn kìa, nó đang ăn từ tay cháu! Ông: Ngồi thật yên nhé. Đừng cử động ngón tay. Theo: Lông nó màu xám, có chút tím tím. Đẹp ghê. Ông: Ừ. Bồ câu là bạn của thành phố. Nhiều người quên điều đó.

questions.prompt: What does Theo ask Grandpa for?
questions.explanation: 'Can I have some bread for the birds?'

questions.prompt: What kind of bread does Grandpa give?
questions.explanation: 'It is old bread from this morning.'

questions.prompt: Why is fresh bread not good for the birds?
questions.explanation: 'Fresh bread is too soft. Old bread is better for the birds.'

questions.prompt: What size pieces should Theo throw?
Where do the pigeons come from?
What is special about one pigeon?
When does Capitaine come to the bench?
questions.explanation: 'He comes to this bench every Sunday.'

questions.prompt: Where does the pigeon eat from at the end?
What does Grandpa tell Theo to do?
options: Stay very still and not move his fingers
questions.explanation: 'Stay very still. Do not move your fingers.'

questions.prompt: What colours are the pigeon's feathers?
questions.explanation: 'His feathers are grey and a little purple.'

questions.prompt: What does Grandpa believe about pigeons?

cloze.template:
Theo: Grandpa, can I have some {{b1}} for the birds?
Grandpa: Of course, Theo. Here is a small bag. It is {{b2}} bread from this morning.
Theo: Why old bread? Is fresh bread bad for them?
Grandpa: Fresh bread is too {{b3}}. Old bread is better for the birds.
Theo: Look! Three {{b4}} already!
Grandpa: Throw {{b5}} pieces. Not big ones. Their beaks are small.
Theo: Now there are {{b6}}! Where are they all coming from?
Grandpa: From the church {{b7}}. They see the bread and they fly down.
Theo: That one has only one {{b8}}!
Grandpa: Yes, his name is {{b9}}. He comes to this bench every {{b10}}.
Theo: Look, he is eating from my hand!
Grandpa: Stay very {{b11}}. Do not move your fingers.
Theo: His feathers are grey and a little purple. He is beautiful.
Grandpa: Yes. Pigeons are {{b12}} of cities. Many people forget that.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some people love city pigeons; others find them a problem. What do you think? Are pigeons good or bad for cities?
